text: The Registration module shall provide an interface for citizens to register complaints with the police.
The Registration module shall facilitate information exchange between police and complainants.
The Investigation module shall automate tasks performed after initial registration entries to facilitate the investigation process.
The Prosecution module shall provide a platform for police personnel to record entries of court interactions.
The Search module shall allow police personnel to execute basic and advanced searches on cases using criteria including person, type of crime, modus operandi, and property.
The Search module shall allow users to customize result views by criminal, accused, or case.
The Citizen Interface module shall provide a conduit for information exchange and acknowledgements between citizens and police units.
The Navigation module shall provide role-based landing pages reflecting assigned cases, alerts, and pending tasks.
The Configuration module shall allow authorized users to create, update, and delete state-specific data including acts, sections, castes, tribes, and property information.
The system shall provide detailed context-sensitive help material for all user interfaces in both offline and online modes.
The system shall provide an interface for users to log and track defects or enhancement requests.
The system shall send automated alerts via email or SMS to the user when actions are taken on reported alerts.
The system shall provide help-desk users with reports on defects categorized by status and age.
The system shall maintain an unalterable audit trail capturing all create, read, update, and delete actions on critical entities including cases, suspects, and property.
The audit trail shall automatically record the identity of the initiating user and the date and time of the event.
The system shall maintain audit trail data for at least the life of the case to which it refers.
The system shall allow authorized users to export audit trails for specified cases without affecting the stored data.
The system shall capture and store unauthorized access attempts in the audit trail.
The system shall provide audit reports organized by case, user, and chronological sequence.
The system shall restrict case access to specified users or user groups.
The system shall implement role-based access control for all internal functionalities.
The system shall restrict the setup of user profiles and group allocations to administrative users only.
The system shall restrict changes to security attributes and password management to super-users only.
The system shall exclude records from search results if the user does not have the required access rights.
The system shall provide meaningful error messages accompanied by explanatory text and suggested user actions.
The system shall employ a consistent set of user interface rules to provide a common look and feel.
The user interface shall be compatible with specialist software and guidelines for users with special needs.
The system shall support user-definable persistent defaults for data entry fields.
The system shall allow users to customize the graphical user interface including menus, screen layouts, colors, and fonts.
The user interface shall comply with ISO 9241 standards for ergonomics and accessibility.
The system shall provide alternative textual descriptions for all non-text media objects.
The system shall provide clear navigation indicators showing the user's current position within the application structure.
The system shall minimize the number of navigation steps required to reach specific content.
The system shall minimize vertical scrolling and avoid horizontal scrolling.
The system shall be designed for input device independence to allow operation via keyboard.
The application shall be designed to achieve acceptable download times across the expected range of network bandwidths.
The system shall support input and output of information using localized units of measurement, currency, and date formats.
The system shall provide a printer-friendly version for documents that are long or use complex layouts.
The system shall provide a site map showing the structure of the application.
The system shall be available to users during specified hours and weekdays with planned downtime not exceeding a defined threshold per three-month period.
The system shall be restorable within a specified number of hours following a software or hardware failure.
The system shall return search results for a simple search within 5-8 seconds.
The system shall return search results for an advanced search within 10-15 seconds.
The system shall be designed using a 3-tier architecture that separates presentation, business, and data-access logic.
The system shall support a multilingual user interface.
The system shall maintain operational data integrity in the event of equipment or communication network failure.
The system shall allow critical functionality to operate in offline mode.
The system shall use Open Standards and Service Oriented Architecture (SOA) principles.
The system shall use HTTPS over SSL for all network communications.
The system shall prevent SQL injection and cross-site scripting through input validation and parameterized queries.
The system shall perform only soft deletes by tagging rows for deletion instead of hard deletes.
The system shall fetch search results from the database in batches of 10 or 20 records.

The UUIS shall provide a Web interface that allows users to access and manage the integrated inventory.
The IUFA shall guarantee secure access to the data from outside the university during working hours.
The UUIS shall allow users to transfer assets.
The UUIS shall allow users to edit assets.
The UUIS shall allow users to modify all asset fields except for IDs.
The UUIS shall allow the use of a bulk entry file for modifying assets.
The UUIS shall allow users to add inventory assets.
The UUIS shall allow the use of bulk entry to add multiple assets.
The UUIS shall allow authorized users to create requests to borrow an asset or reserve a space.
The UUIS shall allow inventory users to check returned assets and update the inventory.
The UUIS shall allow IT group members to create a new space and modify floor structures upon receipt of an exception request.
The UUIS shall allow authorized users to display and approve pending requests.
The UUIS shall notify the user by email when a request is treated.
The UUIS shall update the inventory when a user receives a requested asset.
The UUIS shall authenticate all users via a username and a password.
The UUIS shall allow administrative level users to delegate authorized actions to other users.
The UUIS shall generate asset reports by location.
The UUIS shall generate request reports.
The UUIS shall generate user permission reports.
The UUIS shall enable a learning time between 2 to 4 hours maximum for users.
The Web interface shall use consistent terminology for users with basic internet and office experience.
The application shall be available during all working hours.
The application shall be installable on Microsoft and Unix platforms.
The Web application shall run on IE, Firefox, Chrome, Opera, and Safari browsers.
The UUIS shall restrict database server access to IT team members locally.
The UUIS shall terminate any query that exceeds 1 minute of execution time.
The UUIS shall execute backup operations periodically.
The UUIS shall be designed to facilitate future evolution and maintenance operations.
The software shall maintain all components under a version control system.
The software shall include facilities for nearly automatic inclusion into the Gemini software environment.
The software shall restrict access to the system according to the current operational level.
The software shall provide monitoring access as a read-only case that does not affect the performance of ongoing observations.
The software shall support remote operations through a distributed environment.
The software shall support team observing with multiple observers at different sites.
The software shall restrict specific operations to specific remote sites.
The software shall ensure that no single point of control for the telescope exists for observing astronomers.
The software shall provide an automatic sequencer for observing operations.
The software shall support the sorting and resequencing of the observation queue.
The software shall provide a telescope simulator for testing science programs.
The software shall support a command syntax that is consistent across workstation and IOC software.
The software shall respond to a common set of commands for status testing and version inquiries.
The software shall adopt a uniform ACK/NAK protocol across all systems.
The software shall support command timeouts at approximately 500 msec.
The software shall ensure that handshaking of commands between IOCs occurs within 100-200 msec.
The LAN shall support a transfer rate of 20-40 Mbits/second.
The software shall store astronomical data using a standard compressed format.
The software shall retain 7 days of data produced by the largest instrument.
The software shall transmit rough video images every 0.5 sec.
The software shall implement an Access Mode Allocation system to protect against accidental interference.
The software shall accept or reject every command within 2 sec.
The software shall update local status displays within 4 sec.
The software shall log and fix all software bugs after detection.
The software shall provide built-in test (BIT) facilities to verify system performance.
The software shall notify users of specific faults including origin and problem description.
The software shall allow control transfer between user stations via a software reconfiguration procedure.
The software shall be written as machine-independent portable code for non-hardware-specific tasks.
The software shall verify the consistency of all software component versions at boot time.
The software shall support a total system downtime of no more than 2%.
The software shall perform range and validity checking before executing any input command.
The software shall recover or reconfigure within 5 minutes of an error condition.
The software shall bring the system to a safe state upon notification of danger.
The software shall provide a network gateway acting as a firewall for the astronomical database.
The software shall support database access times in the range of 2-3 msec.
The software shall use the Ward/Mellor approach for real-time systems development.
The development operating system shall be POSIX-compliant UNIX System V Release 4.
The software shall use Tcl/Tk as the principle language for developing Graphical User Interfaces.
The IOCs shall use the VxWorks real-time operating system.
The software shall use the DARPA TCP/IP communication protocol over LANs and the WAN.
The THEMAS system shall read the initialization file and derive definitions from the initialization data in the file.
The THEMAS system shall use the information contained in the initialization data file to determine which heating and cooling units are part of the system.
Each thermostat shall have a unique identifier by which that thermostat is identified in the THEMAS system.
The THEMAS system shall read thermostat identifiers from the initialization file and load them into the system during the initialization process.
The THEMAS system shall read the maximum number of concurrently running units from the initialization file and store it in the system.
The THEMAS system shall read the initialization file and establish the trigger value from the data in that file.
The THEMAS system shall ensure the temperature reported by a given thermostat does not exceed a maximum deviation value of 3 degrees Fahrenheit.
The THEMAS system shall read the initialization file and establish the overtemperature value from the data in that file.
The THEMAS system shall read the initialization file and establish the valid temperature range from the data in it.
The THEMAS system shall turn off all heating and cooling units when initialized.
The THEMAS system shall read the current temperature values and current temperature settings of each thermostat during initialization.
The THEMAS system shall issue a request to turn on the appropriate unit if initialization settings reflect a need for heating or cooling.

The THEMAS system shall identify a current temperature value as an invalid temperature and output an invalid temperature status if the value is strictly less than the lower value or strictly greater than the upper value of the valid temperature range.
The THEMAS system shall output a valid temperature status if the current temperature value is within the valid temperature range.

The THEMAS system shall identify a temperature setting as an invalid temperature and output an invalid temperature status if the setting value is strictly less than the lower value or strictly greater than the upper value of the valid temperature range.
The THEMAS system shall report temperatures that exceed the overtemperature limits.
The THEMAS system shall recognize a temperature limit has been exceeded if the temperature is less than the Lower Overtemperature Value or greater than the Upper Overtemperature Value.
The THEMAS system shall activate the appropriate heating or cooling unit when the current temperature value exceeds the current temperature setting by a pre-defined amount.
The THEMAS system shall output a request to turn off both the heating unit and the cooling unit if the current temperature is between the Lower Trigger Value and the Upper Trigger Value inclusive.
The THEMAS system shall output a request to turn on the heating unit if the temperature is between the Lower Overtemperature Value and the Lower Trigger Value.
The THEMAS system shall output a request to turn on the cooling unit if the temperature is between the Upper Trigger Value and the Upper Overtemperature Value.
The THEMAS system shall limit the number of heating or cooling units that run simultaneously.
The THEMAS system shall maintain the ON/OFF status of each heating and cooling unit.
The THEMAS system shall add a request to turn on a unit to a LIFO queue if the maximum number of heating or cooling units is already running.
The THEMAS system shall remove one request from the LIFO queue and check the thermostat state when a request to turn off a unit is received and the queue is not empty.
The THEMAS system shall record an event when a request for a heating or cooling unit to be turned on is denied.
The THEMAS system shall generate a control signal for a heating or cooling unit once a request to turn it on or off is approved.
The THEMAS system shall generate an event each time a change is made to the status of a heating or cooling unit.
The THEMAS system shall output a continuous series of alternating 500 Hz and 700 Hz beeps if an invalid temperature is detected.
The THEMAS system shall output a continuous series of alternating 1000 Hz and 1500 Hz beeps if a temperature limit has been exceeded.
The THEMAS system shall continue alarm beeps until the supervisor manually resets the alarm through the interface window.
The THEMAS system shall record an alarm event each time an alarm is requested.
The THEMAS system shall record each event by a description and the current system time in a Microsoft Access database.
The THEMAS system shall provide the supervisor a mechanism to change the temperature setting of any thermostat.
The THEMAS system shall provide the supervisor a mechanism to select between an operational history report and a statistical summary report.
The THEMAS system shall provide reports in the form of an ASCII file.
The THEMAS system shall run on the Microsoft Windows NT operating system.
The THEMAS system shall provide a prototype of a simulated thermostat for verification and validation of system reliability.
XML Services shall be segmented.
XML Services shall provide infrastructure services for mission and support applications using XML technology.
XML Services shall provide data management functions for access to distributed database management systems.
Data management services shall have the capability of being tuned by on-site personnel in the fixed mode of operation.
XML Services shall redefine or reset names of connect descriptors to database server instances in a dynamic environment.
The XML Services shall reset session time-out values in a degraded communications environment.
Session time-out values shall be user definable.
XML Services shall support managing various types of data and database architectures in Maintenance Mode.
The data management services shall provide production-equivalent processing in Training Mode.
XML Services shall receive XML registry forms.
XML Services shall submit metadata and related information resource artifacts.
XML Services shall produce and display submittal forms as part of the Information Resource Submittal Package from the web.
XML Services shall download Information Resource Submittal Package from the web.
XML Services shall submit prescribed metadata related to information resource type and status code.
XML Services shall submit metadata by an on-line interactive process.
XML Services shall submit metadata by an off-line and interactive batch process.
XML Services shall parse submitted XML Registry specification forms.
XML Services shall populate the XML Registry database.
XML Services shall modify specified terms and definitions of metadata.
XML Services shall associate Information Resources specified in Appendix A.
XML Services shall assemble registered information resources to form new components.
XML Services shall notify changes in Information Resources to authorized producers.
XML Services shall provide a capability to post planned changes to a registry.
XML Services shall approve and reject submissions.
XML Services shall forward requests to different registries.
XML Services shall provide the capability to view a Tag origin relationship.
XML Services shall provide the capability to view a complex Container Tag parent/child relationship.
XML Services shall provide the capability to view a tag data type.
XML Services shall provide the capability to view an Information resource versioning relationship.
XML Services shall view XML Tags via a tree hierarchy structure or tabular format.
XML Services shall view XML Tags via multiple search options.
XML Services shall provide capabilities to download the XML Tags.
XML Services shall process ANSI standard SQL as specified in FIPS PUB 127-2.
XML Services shall establish rules for semantic understanding of terms in an XML document.
XML Services shall provide a capability to subscribe for notification of changes to Communities of Interest.
XML Services shall create and manage usernames and superusers.
XML Services shall monitor changes to data models recorded in the Registry.
XML Services shall validate tags against a style guide wizard.
XML Services shall generate a document that includes a schema.
XML Services shall develop a scheme for dynamic cross-referencing of XML objects.
XML Services shall check XML document conformance for well-formedness and validation.
XML Services shall disassemble documents to materialize query results into different tree structures.
XML Services shall transmit sufficient metadata to construct semantically-valid XSL views.
XML Services shall provide standard APIs for Registry Services as specified in Appendix B.
The Registry software shall be portable.
The Registry software shall execute on COE-compliant platforms.
The XS software shall be delivered in accordance with DII COE guidelines.
The MultiMahjong system shall consist of a MultiMahjongServer and a MultiMahjongClient program.
The MultiMahjong system shall allow up to 4 players to play Mahjong over a TCP/IP network.
The MultiMahjongClient program shall allow 1 player to play in a stand-alone mode.
The MultiMahjong system shall provide computer opponents to ensure every game has 4 players.
The MultiMahjongServer shall reside on a central computer connected to a TCP/IP network with a unique IP address.
The MultiMahjongServer shall allow and communicate with connections from MultiMahjongClients using IP.
The MultiMahjongServer shall relay data received from one MultiMahjongClient to other requesting MultiMahjongClients.
The MultiMahjongClient shall allow the user to create a new multi-player game.
The MultiMahjongClient shall allow the user to create a new single-player game.
The MultiMahjongClient shall allow the user to join an existing multi-player game.
The MultiMahjongClient shall allow the user to quit the program when no game is in progress.
The MultiMahjongClient shall require the user to enter a name and choose an icon when creating or joining a game.
The MultiMahjongClient shall limit the total number of players to 4, including human and computer opponents.
The MultiMahjongClient shall allow the user to set the score limit for winning a hand.
The MultiMahjongClient shall send game initialization and join information to the MultiMahjongServer.
The MultiMahjongClient shall perform the processing for any required Computer Opponents.
The MultiMahjongClient shall retrieve a list of available games from the MultiMahjongServer.
The MultiMahjongClient shall retrieve player names and seating positions from the MultiMahjongServer.
The MultiMahjongClient shall allow moves only according to the Chinese rules of Mahjong.
The MultiMahjongClient shall inform the user when it is their turn to pick up a tile.
The MultiMahjongClient shall display when another player picks up a tile from the wall.
The MultiMahjongClient shall display when another player exposes a set of tiles.
The MultiMahjongClient shall display when another player discards a tile.
The MultiMahjongClient shall inform the user when they are eligible to Chow, Pung, Kong, or Mahjong.
The MultiMahjongClient shall allow the user to pick up a tile from the wall if it is their turn.
The MultiMahjongClient shall allow the user to discard a tile after picking one up.
The MultiMahjongClient shall inform the user if another player is fishing for Mahjong.
The MultiMahjongClient shall inform the user if another player goes Mahjong.
The MultiMahjongClient shall inform the user if the hand ends in a draw.
The game shall end when all 4 rounds have been played or if the user chooses to end the game.
The MultiMahjongServer shall inform all connected MultiMahjongClients when a game has ended.
The Computer Opponent shall not have access to concealed tiles in other players' hands or tiles in the wall.
The Computer Opponent shall have access to the number of tiles remaining in the wall and in players' hands.
The Computer Opponent shall be able to read all exposed or revealed hands.
The Computer Opponent shall be able to pick up tiles, discard tiles, reveal Kongs, and perform Chow, Pung, or Mahjong actions.
The MultiMahjongClient shall provide a dialogue box reporting the nature of the error when a nonfatal error occurs.
The MultiMahjongClient shall allow the user to continue the current game after a nonfatal error.
The MultiMahjongClient shall display a dialogue box and terminate execution when a fatal error occurs.
The MultiMahjong system shall be written using JDK 1.2.
The MultiMahjong system shall follow the Sun Microsystems Java coding standard.
The MultiMahjong software shall run on machines capable of running a Java Virtual Machine supporting JDK 1.2.
The Computer Opponent shall respond within 1 minute in both single and multi-player modes.
The MultiMahjongServer shall handle requests for up to 10 simultaneous games.
The MultiMahjongClient shall calculate possible Chow, Pung, Kong, or Mahjong moves within 5 seconds of a discard.
The MultiMahjongServer shall be operated via a command prompt without a graphical user interface.
The MultiMahjongClient shall provide a graphical user interface interactable via mouse and keyboard.
The MultiMahjongClient GUI shall be contained within a screen resolution of 800 x 600 pixels.
The MultiMahjongClient GUI shall display all graphics in 16-bit color.
The MultiMahjongClient Main Window shall display the user's tiles and the tile wall.
The MultiMahjongClient Main Window shall display buttons for game actions and grey them out when inactive.
The MultiMahjong product shall include an administrator's manual for the server in electronic format.
The MultiMahjong product shall include a user's manual for the client in both hard copy and electronic format.
The TCS shall consist of the TCS workstation Hardware Configuration Items (HWCIs), TCS Computer Software Configuration Items (CSCIs), and additional TCS Support HWCIs and CSCIs.
All TCS HWCIs and CSCIs shall be Year 2000 compliant.
The TCS shall provide command and control of the payload Air Vehicle (AV), and data link.
The TCS shall interface with and export payload data to military supported units via external tactical communications systems and C4I systems.
The TCS shall support five levels of UAV interaction ranging from secondary imagery receipt to full function control from takeoff to landing.
The TCS shall provide software capabilities and hardware configurations necessary to fulfill operational tasking requirements across the five levels of interaction.
The TCS states of operation shall include Startup, Operation, and Shutdown.
The TCS states shall not exist concurrently.
The TCS shall enter the Startup State upon application of power.
The Startup State shall be comprised of Normal Startup Mode and Recovery Startup Mode.
The TCS shall enter the Normal Startup Mode when the TCS software is terminated normally.
The TCS shall enter the Recovery Startup Mode when the software is halted due to an unplanned power interruption or abnormal program termination.
The TCS shall determine which of the five levels of interaction are achievable by the TCS configuration during startup.
The TCS shall prohibit levels of interaction higher than those achievable by a particular configuration.
The TCS shall inform the operator if the operator attempts to execute a function prohibited as a result of the determined level of interaction.
The TCS shall initialize the system to place it in the Operations State within 60 seconds from the time power is supplied and the application is launched during Normal Startup Mode.
The TCS shall provide the system functionality to resume the Operations State within 45 seconds during Recovery Startup Mode.
The TCS shall be capable of automatically recording system state data and interface communications to support event reconstruction.
The TCS shall prompt the operator to select the type of recovery to be executed following an abnormal termination.
The Operations State shall consist of three modes: normal operations, training operations, and maintenance operations.
The Operations State modes shall not exist concurrently.
The operator shall have the capability to command the system to the Shutdown State from all modes under the Operations State.
The TCS hardware and software shall execute periodic Fault Detection/Location (FD/L) while in the Normal Operations Mode and Training Mode.
The TCS shall support concurrent operation of Mission Planning and Mission Control functions during Normal Operations Mode.
The TCS shall be placed in a condition where power can be removed without impacting operations or causing damage to the system upon selection of a shutdown command.
The TCS shall provide the functionality to allow the operator to generate a UAV mission plan.
The TCS shall have the functionality to receive and process UAV mission plans from service specific mission planning systems.
The TCS shall be capable of storing a minimum of 500 mission plans under unique names.
The Mission Planning function shall provide a graphical user interface that allows defining waypoints on a map based display using a pointing device.
The TCS shall provide the capability to compute the range and bearing between two geographic positions on the map display.
The TCS shall permit dynamic mission and payload retasking during all phases of operational mission execution.
The TCS shall automatically check the validity of the intended mission plan prior to being uploaded.
The TCS shall provide the capability for the operator to define the desired AV route in waypoint format.
The TCS shall provide the capability to include up to 500 waypoints in each flight route plan.
The TCS shall provide the capability to display mission waypoints and flight path graphically.
The TCS shall present the estimated time of arrival and fuel status at each programmed waypoint to the operator.
The algorithmic accuracy of all flight planning calculations shall not deviate by more than 10% when compared with a validated 6 degree-of-freedom air vehicle simulator.
The TCS shall provide the operator with a clearly indicated map scale.
The TCS shall display an icon indicating AV position when receiving downlink from the desired AV.
The TCS shall be capable of displaying the positions of icons in Lat/Lon and Military Grid Reference System (MGRS) formats.
The TCS shall transmit command and control information to the AV via the uplink.
The TCS shall receive AV telemetry and payload information via downlink from the AV.
The TCS shall provide the capability to pass control of an AV to another TCS.
The TCS shall notify the operator when AV performance parameters are out of limits.
The TCS shall support operation of the AV via all flight modes including manual controls.
The TCS shall provide the functionality to control the Air Data Terminal (ADT) antenna and transmitter.
The TCS shall compute the estimated position of the AV during Loss of Link (LOL) based upon the last known position.
The TCS shall display a LOL timer to the operator upon initiation of a LOL onset.
The TCS shall provide the system functionality necessary to record data obtained via the data link.
The TCS shall be capable of automatically selecting the proper mode of operation for the selected data terminal.
The TCS shall have the functionality to process payload product data from Electro Optical (EO) and Infrared (IR) payloads.
The TCS shall be able to store up to 24 hours of payload data.
The TCS shall provide an interface between the TCS and an external hard copy printer.
The TCS shall implement an AV Standard Interface to ensure communications with the selected AV.
The TCS shall be capable of restoring power in sufficient time to avoid loss of air vehicle control during power outages.
The TCS shall be designed such that no single hardware failure or software error results in an unsafe command being transmitted to the air vehicle.
The TCS throughput shall not exceed 50% of the throughput capability delivered over any 10 second period.
The TCS shall provide a 50% spare memory storage capacity over delivered storage used.
The TCS software shall provide a windows based graphic operator interface.
The TCS software shall be non-proprietary and have unlimited data rights.
The TCS shall provide the functionality to have a maximum delay time of 1 second from operator command to system acknowledgement.
The TCS shall be capable of operating continuously in functional Operation Mode for a minimum of 72 hours.

The TCS shall support 5 levels of UAV interaction: Level 1 (secondary imagery/data receipt), Level 2 (direct imagery/data receipt), Level 3 (payload control), Level 4 (AV control less launch/recovery), and Level 5 (full control).
The TCS shall consist of workstation Hardware Configuration Items (HWCIs), Computer Software Configuration Items (CSCIs), and additional support HWCIs and CSCIs.
All TCS HWCIs and CSCIs shall be Year 2000 compliant.

The TCS shall meet the applicable capability and characteristic criteria established by the Operational Requirements Document (ORD) for the RQ-1A Predator and the Close Range-Tactical Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (CR-TUAV).
The TCS shall provide software capabilities and hardware configurations necessary to fulfill operational tasking across the 5 levels of interaction.
The TCS shall be interoperable with Electro-Optic/Infrared (EO/IR) and Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) payloads as identified in the Interoperable TCS Payloads table.
The TCS states of operation shall include Startup, Operation, and Shutdown.
The TCS states shall not exist concurrently.
The TCS shall enter the Startup State upon application of power.
The Startup State shall comprise the Normal Startup Mode and the Recovery Startup Mode.
The TCS shall enter Normal Startup Mode when the software is terminated normally.
The TCS shall enter Recovery Startup Mode when the software is halted due to unplanned power interruption or abnormal termination.
The TCS shall determine which of the 5 levels of interaction are achievable by the configuration during startup.
The TCS shall prohibit levels of interaction higher than those achievable by a particular configuration.
The TCS shall inform the operator if they attempt to execute a function prohibited by the determined level of interaction.
The TCS shall initialize the system to enter the Operations State within 60 seconds from the time power is supplied and the application is launched in Normal Startup Mode.
The TCS shall provide functionality to resume the Operations State within 45 seconds in Recovery Startup Mode.
The TCS shall automatically record system state data and interface communications to support event reconstruction.
The TCS shall prompt the operator to select the type of recovery to be executed after abnormal termination.
The Operations State shall include three modes: normal operations, training operations, and maintenance operations.
The Operations State modes shall not exist concurrently.

The TCS hardware and software shall execute periodic Fault Detection/Location (FD/L) while in Normal Operations and Training Modes.

The TCS shall enter the Shutdown State upon selection of a shutdown command to allow power removal without impacting operations or damaging the system.

The TCS shall be capable of storing a minimum of 500 mission plans under unique names.

The TCS shall compute range and bearing between two geographic positions on the map display.
The TCS shall automatically check the validity of mission plans (altitude, fuel, data link constraints) prior to being uploaded.
The TCS shall notify the operator of discrepancies found during mission plan validation checks.
The TCS shall provide the capability to override validation faults after operator acknowledgment.
The TCS shall allow up to 500 waypoints in each flight route plan.
The TCS shall compute the Air Vehicle (AV) flight range based on remaining fuel at every waypoint and every 5 minutes.

The TCS shall display icon positions in Lat/Lon, Universal Transverse Mercator (UTM), and Military Grid Reference System (MGRS) formats.
The TCS shall transmit command and control information to the AV and receive telemetry and payload data via the data link.
The TCS shall provide control functionality for two MAE air vehicles flying Beyond Line of Sight (BLOS).
The TCS shall notify the operator when AV performance parameters are out of limits.
The TCS shall provide the capability to pass control of an AV to another TCS and receive control from another TCS.
The TCS shall display a Loss of Link (LOL) timer to the operator and compute estimated AV position during LOL.
The TCS shall process payload product data from Electro-Optical (EO), Infrared (IR), and Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) payloads.
The TCS shall be able to store up to 24 hours of payload data.
The TCS shall be in compliance with National Imagery Transmission Format (NITF) Version 2.0 and United States Imagery Standards (USIS) when processing payload imagery.
The TCS shall have a built-in text entry capability to annotate textual information on imagery.
The TCS shall compute range and bearing between two geographic positions located on the payload imagery display.
The TCS shall provide functionality to determine target locations within the payload field of view and express them in military coordinates.
The TCS shall provide functionality to develop an estimate of the error in computed target coordinates.
The TCS shall be capable of entering DII-COE compliant C4I networks.
The TCS shall automatically check digital messages for errors and flag uncorrectable messages to the operator.
The TCS shall restrict operator access to software upgrades and programmable parameter modification via password protection.
The TCS shall interface with standard DoD tactical radios (VHF , UHF) and military/commercial satellite communications equipment.
The TCS shall incorporate an intercom system compatible with service-specific voice communication systems for verbal operator communication.
The TCS shall comply with MIL-STD-882C for system safety program requirements.
The TCS shall provide appropriate cautions and warnings to the operator if the air vehicle deviates into an unsafe flight regime.
The TCS shall be designed such that no single hardware failure or software error results in an unsafe command being transmitted to the air vehicle.
The TCS hardware shall be scaleable or modular to meet varying service needs.
The TCS throughput shall not exceed 50% of the throughput capability delivered over any 10 second period.
The TCS shall be capable of providing a 50% spare memory storage capacity over delivered storage used.
The TCS software shall be non-proprietary and have unlimited data rights.
The TCS shall provide functionality to have a maximum delay time of 1 second from operator command to system acknowledgment and response.
The TCS shall be capable of operating continuously in functional Operation Mode for a minimum of 72 hours.
The TCS shall be configurable for sea ground or air transport in 2 hours or less.

The HATS-GUI shall facilitate the execution of transformation language programs.

The Libra Scheduler shall manage sequential and embarrassingly parallel batch jobs.
The Libra Scheduler shall run on a homogenous Linux cluster.
The Libra Scheduler shall prioritize jobs based on user-specified budget and deadline constraints.
The Libra Scheduler shall allocate CPU time according to user utility.
The Libra Scheduler shall enforce resource allocations proportional to user priority using the stride scheduling algorithm.

The Libra Scheduler shall retrieve and parse job details including Job Id and Standalone Execution time.

The Libra Scheduler shall notify the user if a job is accepted or rejected based on feasibility.
The Libra Scheduler shall provide a Linux man page for on-line user documentation.

The wcc386 compiler shall support a new -elf command line switch to specify ELF object file format.
The wcc386 compiler shall support a new -zpic command line switch to enable Position-Independent Code generation.
The CG386 code generator shall exclude the EBX register from normal register allocation when PIC generation is enabled.
The compiler shall generate a Position-Independent function prologue that computes the absolute address of the Global Offset Table into the EBX register.
The compiler shall save the EBX, ESI, and EDI registers on the stack during the function prologue for both PIC and PDC.
The compiler shall restore all saved registers from the stack during the function epilogue.
The compiler shall generate R_386_PLT32 relocation types for direct function calls when PIC is enabled.
The compiler shall reference global data through offsets in the Global Offset Table using the EBX register as a base.
The compiler shall use R_386_GOTOFF relocations for position-independent references to static data.
The CG386 code generator shall implement ELF object file output by mapping OMF entities to OWL functions.
The OWL library shall be extended to support 386-specific relocation types including R_386_GOT32, R_386_GOTOFF, R_386_GOTPC, and R_386_PLT32.
The wlink linker shall support building ELF shared objects using the 'form ELF DLL' command line option.
The linker shall set the ELF header e_type field to ET_DYN when building shared objects.
The linker shall include a PT_DYNAMIC program header in shared objects to specify dynamic linking information.
The linker shall create a .dynamic section containing the dynamic array tags required by the System V ABI.
The linker shall generate both a normal symbol table (.symtab) and a dynamic symbol table (.dynsym) for shared objects.
The linker shall merge all dynamic relocations into a single relocation table named .rel.dyn for shared objects.
The linker shall build a Global Offset Table (GOT) when R_386_GOT32, R_386_GOTOFF, or R_386_GOTPC relocations are encountered.
The linker shall reserve the first three entries of the Global Offset Table as specified by the 386 ABI.
The linker shall define the symbol *GLOBAL_OFFSET_TABLE* at the base of the .got section.
The linker shall create a Procedure Linkage Table (PLT) when R_386_PLT32 relocations are encountered for STT_FUNC symbols.
The linker shall create R_386_JUMP_SLOT relocations for PLT entries to support lazy binding.
The linker shall support reading existing ELF shared objects using the Object Reading Library (ORL).
The linker shall include a PT_INTERP program header in executables that use shared objects, specifying /lib/ld-linux.so.2 as the path.
The linker shall record shared object dependencies using DT_NEEDED entries in the dynamic section.
The linker must disable 4-byte offset correction for R_386_PC32 relocations when an implicit addend is specified in the ELF file.
The ORL shall treat STT_NOTYPE symbols as normal symbols to prevent fatal linker errors during dietlibc linking.
The linker shall adjust the p_memsz field in the program header to account for .bss section allocation and page alignment.

The ELSS shall process COR correction operations to capture and deliver formatted correction requests.

The ELSS shall transmit data immediately after the last fishing operation has been completed.
The ELSS shall transmit data immediately on departing port.
The ELSS shall transmit data immediately after a transhipment.
The ELSS shall transmit data immediately on completion of the Landing declaration.
